preamble: The voiceless velar lateral affricate [kʟ] simplifies and fronts to a voiceless alveolar plosive [t]. This shift represents a significant reduction in articulatory complexity, moving from a complex velar-lateral release to a simple alveolar closure.

examples.note: Dialectal simplification or historical development from complex lateral affricates in certain Tlingit speech communities.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
